data.title: Legislators, senior officials and managers
data.label_el: Νομοθέτες, ανώτερα διοικητικά και διευθυντικά στελέχη
data.description: Leadership, coordination, policy, and organizational decision-making roles across the economy.
data.exposure_rationale: These roles are highly exposed on planning, reporting, and information-heavy tasks, but less exposed on negotiation, accountability, and people leadership.

data.title: Professionals
data.description: Highly skilled professional occupations such as engineers, doctors, lawyers, teachers, and analysts.
data.exposure_rationale: A large share of this work is digital or document-heavy, so AI can reshape analysis, drafting, and research, even where human judgment remains central.

data.title: Technicians and associate professionals
data.label_el: Τεχνολόγοι, τεχνικοί και ασκούντες συναφή επαγγέλματα
data.description: Technical support and applied-professional roles bridging theory and hands-on execution.
data.exposure_rationale: AI is likely to automate diagnostics, documentation, and some analysis, but many tasks still require fieldwork, equipment, and human oversight.

data.title: Clerks
data.description: Administrative, records, customer support, and clerical roles concentrated on office processes.
data.exposure_rationale: Routine document handling, coordination, and data entry are among the most exposed workflows to automation and AI copilots.

data.title: Service workers and shop and market sale workers
data.label_el: Απασχολούμενοι στην παροχή υπηρεσιών και πωλητές
data.description: Frontline service, hospitality, retail, and sales occupations with strong person-to-person interaction.
data.exposure_rationale: AI can assist scheduling, training, and sales support, but the core work still depends heavily on physical presence and direct customer interaction.

data.title: Skilled agricultural and fishery workers
data.label_el: Ειδικευμένοι γεωργοί, κτηνοτρόφοι, δασοκόμοι και αλιείς
data.description: Skilled occupations in agriculture, livestock, forestry, and fishing tied to land, animals, and physical production.
data.exposure_rationale: Digital tools can improve planning and monitoring, but the work remains constrained by physical environments and field operations.

data.title: Craft and related trade workers
data.label_el: Ειδικευμένοι τεχνίτες και ασκούντες συναφή επαγγέλματα
data.description: Skilled manual trades across construction, repair, fabrication, and installation work.
data.exposure_rationale: The core value of these occupations is still physical execution, site adaptation, and manual skill, which limits direct AI substitution.

data.title: Plant and machine operators and assembler
data.label_el: Χειριστές εγκαταστάσεων και μηχανημάτων και συναρμολογητές
data.description: Machine operation, production-line, transport-equipment, and assembly work in industrial settings.
data.exposure_rationale: Automation pressure is meaningful, but physical equipment supervision and industrial constraints still keep exposure below office-heavy roles.

data.title: Elementary occupations
data.description: Basic manual, support, and low-specialization occupations across many sectors.
data.exposure_rationale: Many tasks are repetitive, but they often still require physical execution in the real world rather than purely digital work.

data.title: Other unclassified persons
data.description: A residual published category for people not assigned to the main occupational group structure.
data.exposure_rationale: This is not a coherent occupation family, so any exposure score should be treated only as a neutral placeholder.

Wholesale and retail trade; repair of motor vehicles and motorcycles
Public administration and defence; compulsory social security
